mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, compressing corporate profit margins and triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts signal a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, forcing equity markets to price in lower future cash flows and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active rate normalization, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of contracting consumer credit on equity valuations, producing sharp downward moves in the S&P 500 over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when both rates rise and credit contracts, or the joint signal's predictive accuracy falls below 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expansion increases aggregate corporate revenue expectations, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are not significantly higher during GDP growth periods compared to contraction periods (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC drops below 0.04 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.5% year-over-year, inflation expectations detach from central bank targets, forcing equity markets to discount future earnings at higher risk premiums and depressing forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline when inflation breaches the threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with stagnant industrial output indicates a structural shift toward services and rising input costs, squeezing corporate margins and driving negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the cross-sectional correlation between the interaction term and forward returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle environment characterized by peak housing activity, a subsequent decline in housing starts reliably precedes broader economic slowdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings revisions turn negative.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral following consecutive housing start declines, or the predictive power of housing momentum drops below 0.03 Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.
